description: Adversarial invariant for database-scope cleanup: generic DBMS belongs to the early-1960s database-management lineage, while relational DBMS and SQL stay anchored to 1970 and 1974 respectively.

checks.reason: Generic DBMS should stay in the early-1960s IDS/database-management lineage.
Relational DBMS should stay anchored to Codd's 1970 relational-model publication, not the generated 1948 placeholder.
Relational DBMS is a later data-model branch of general database-management systems.
SQL/SEQUEL should stay anchored to the 1974 IBM SEQUEL paper.
Relational query languages require the relational database model they query.
Early HIS used database management but should not imply relational DBMS.
HIS database support should route through generic DBMS.
Early EHR systems should not be forced through relational DBMS.
EHR structured records need database-management capability without a relational-specific claim.
1960s expert systems predate relational DBMS and are rule-based software systems.
Expert systems should route through software implementation rather than relational database technology.
ATMs should stay anchored to the 1967 Barclays cash dispenser chronology.
This node is sourced to Random Forests and should not use the generated 1958 ML placeholder.
This node is sourced to support-vector networks and should not use the generated 1958 ML placeholder.
